summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of the CombineFileInputFormat class is to create splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool.

CORE LOGIC:
The code creates splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool. It does this by iterating over all the files in the input set and processing them in a single iteration. For each file, it creates a OneFileInfo object that contains information about the file and its blocks. It then creates splits for all the blocks in the file, using the createSplits method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a JobContext object as input, which contains the configuration and input paths. It returns a list of InputSplit objects, which represent the splits that will be used by the MapReduce job.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the FileInputFormat class and its methods, as well as the CombineFileSplit class and its methods. It also uses the CompressionCodecFactory class and its methods to check if a file is splittable.

The main purpose of this chunk is to create splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool.

CORE LOGIC:
The code creates splits for all files in a pool and for all files that are not in any pool. It does this by iterating over all the files in the input set and processing them in a single iteration. For each file, it creates a OneFileInfo object that contains information about the file and its blocks. It then creates splits for all the blocks in the file, using the createSplits method.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a JobContext object as input, which contains the configuration and input paths. It returns a list of InputSplit objects, which represent the splits that will be used by the MapReduce job.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the FileInputFormat class and its methods, as well as the CombineFileSplit class and its methods. It also uses the CompressionCodecFactory class and its methods to check if a file is splittable.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code chunk is part of the CombineFileInputFormat class, which is used to split the input files into smaller splits based on a maximum size.

CORE LOGIC:
The code chunk iterates over a list of blocks and adds them to a validBlocks list. It also keeps track of the current split size and the list of racks that the blocks belong to. If the current split size exceeds the maximum size, it creates a new input split and adds it to the splits array.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code chunk takes in a list of blocks and a maximum size as input, and it outputs a list of input splits.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code chunk depends on the CombineFileSplit class, which is used to represent an input split. It also depends on the NetworkTopology class, which is used to determine the network topology of the hosts.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- A generic RecordReader that can hand out different RecordReaders for each chunk in a CombineFileSplit.
- A CombineFileSplit can combine data chunks from multiple files.
- This class allows using different RecordReaders for processing these data chunks from different files.

CORE LOGIC:
- The code initializes the RecordReader for the next chunk in the CombineFileSplit.
- It gets a RecordReader for the idx-th chunk and sets up some helper config variables.
- It initializes the RecordReader for the first RecordReader by the MapTask.
- It is responsible for initializing subsequent RecordReaders.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The code takes a CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and Class as inputs.
- It returns a boolean value indicating whether there are more key-value pairs to read.
- It outputs the current key and value.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The code depends on the CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and RecordReader classes.
- It also depends on the Configuration class from the Hadoop framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is a wrapper for a RecordReader that handles a single file split. It delegates most of the methods to the wrapped instance.

CORE LOGIC:
The constructor takes in a FileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and an index. It creates a FileSplit based on the index and delegates the RecordReader creation to the input format. The initialize method checks if the file split is valid and initializes the delegate RecordReader. The nextKeyValue, getCurrentKey, getCurrentValue, getProgress, and close methods are delegated to the delegate RecordReader.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The constructor takes in a FileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and an index. It creates a FileSplit based on the index and delegates the RecordReader creation to the input format. The initialize method takes in an InputSplit and TaskAttemptContext and initializes the delegate RecordReader. The nextKeyValue, getCurrentKey, getCurrentValue, getProgress, and close methods are delegated to the delegate RecordReader.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the FileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext, and RecordReader classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to define a class called CombineFileSplit that represents a sub-collection of input files. It provides functionality for splitting input files into smaller sets and keeping track of the start offsets, lengths, and locations of the files within the split.

CORE LOGIC:

The code defines a class with various attributes and methods for handling input file splits. It includes attributes for the paths of the input files, start offsets, lengths, and locations of the files within the split. It also includes methods for initializing the split, getting the length of the split, getting the start offsets, lengths, and paths of the files, and reading/writing the split data to/from a DataInput/DataOutput stream.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The class provides methods for reading and writing the split data to/from a DataInput/DataOutput stream. It also provides methods for getting the length of the split, the start offsets, lengths, and paths of the files, and the locations where the split resides.

DEPENDENCIES:

The class depends on the following classes and interfaces:

- org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience
- org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
- org.apache.hadoop.io.Text
- org.apache.hadoop.io.Writable
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.InputSplit
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.RecordReader

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code chunk is an input format that is a CombineFileInputFormat-equivalent for SequenceFileInputFormat.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this code chunk is to create a CombineFileRecordReader that can be used in a CombineFileInputFormat-equivalent for SequenceFileInputFormat.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The inputs to this code chunk are an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext. The output is a RecordReader.

DEPENDENCIES:
The dependencies of this code chunk are the CombineFileInputFormat, SequenceFileInputFormat, CombineFileSplit, and CombineFileRecordReader.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
Combines multiple files into one split for processing.

CORE LOGIC:
Uses a CombineFileRecordReader to read from the combined files.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
Input: CombineFileSplit, TaskAttemptContext
Output: LongWritable, Text

DEPENDENCIES:
TextInputFormat, CombineFileInputFormat, CombineFileRecordReader, LongWritable, Text

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is used to read lines from compressed input splits.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to read lines from compressed input splits,
 handling the special cases where the last block of the split ends in the
 middle of a record delimiter.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The input to this class is a compressed input stream and a configuration
object. The output is a line of text read from the compressed input stream.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the SplitCompressionInputStream class, which is used
to read from compressed input splits. It also depends on the Configuration
class, which is used to configure the behavior of the line reader.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class is an InputFormat that delegates behavior of paths to multiple other InputFormats.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to build a map of InputFormats to Paths, and then for each set of paths that have a common InputFormat and Mapper, add them to the same job and split together.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class takes in a JobContext and returns a list of InputSplits.

DEPENDENCIES:
This class depends on the MultipleInputs class for getting the input format map and mapper type map. It also depends on the ReflectionUtils class for creating new instances of InputFormats and Mappers.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code chunk is a Mapper that delegates behavior of paths to multiple other mappers.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this code chunk is to find the appropriate Mapper from the TaggedInputSplit and then run it.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The input to this code chunk is the TaggedInputSplit, and the output is the result of running the appropriate Mapper.

DEPENDENCIES:
This code chunk depends on the TaggedInputSplit class and the ReflectionUtils class from the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to create a DelegatingRecordReader class that delegates the functionality to the underlying record reader in TaggedInputSplit.

CORE LOGIC:

The code uses ReflectionUtils to instantiate the InputFormat and create a RecordReader from the TaggedInputSplit. It then delegates the functionality to the original RecordReader.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as inputs and returns a DelegatingRecordReader as output.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the InputFormat, InputSplit, RecordReader, TaskAttemptContext, and ReflectionUtils classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The FileInputFormat class is the base class for all file-based InputFormats.
It provides a generic implementation of getSplits(JobContext).

CORE LOGIC:

The getSplits(JobContext) method generates splits for the input files.
It uses the listStatus(JobContext) method to get the list of files and then
processes each file to create splits based on the file size and block size.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The getSplits(JobContext) method takes a JobContext object as input and returns
a list of InputSplit objects.

DEPENDENCIES:

The FileInputFormat class depends on the following classes:

- InputFormat: The base class for all input formats.
- InputSplit: The base class for input splits.
- JobContext: The context object for the job.
- Path: The path to the input file.
- FileSystem: The file system for the input file.
- FileStatus: The status of the input file.
- BlockLocation: The location of the blocks of the input file.
- TokenCache: The cache for delegation tokens.
- Configuration: The configuration object for the job.
- StopWatch: A utility class for measuring time.
- LocatedFileStatusFetcher: A class for fetching located file statuses.
- ReflectionUtils: A utility class for working with reflection.
- InvalidInputException: An exception for invalid input.
- MultiPathFilter: A class for filtering input paths.
- PathFilter: The interface for path filters.
- FileSplit: The class for file splits.
- BlockLocation: The class for block locations.
- LocatedFileStatus: The class for located file statuses.
- FileStatus: The class for file statuses.
- FileSystem: The class for file systems.
- RemoteIterator: The interface for remote iterators.
- InterruptedIOException: The class for interrupted IO exceptions.
- IOException: The base class for IO exceptions.
- IllegalArgumentException: The class for illegal argument exceptions.
- StopWatch: The class for stop watches.
- Configuration: The class for configurations.
- JobContext: The class for job contexts.
- InputSplit: The class for input splits.
- InputFormat: The class for input formats.
- Mapper: The class for mappers.
- TokenCache

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- setInputPaths: sets the list of input paths for the map-reduce job
- addInputPaths: adds the given comma separated paths to the list of inputs for the map-reduce job
- setInputPaths: sets the array of paths as the list of inputs for the map-reduce job
- addInputPath: adds a path to the list of inputs for the map-reduce job

CORE LOGIC:
- setInputPaths and addInputPaths: escape commas in the glob pattern of the given paths
- setInputPaths: sets the input paths for the map-reduce job
- addInputPath: adds the given path to the list of inputs for the map-reduce job

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- setInputPaths: takes a JobContext as input and sets the input paths for the map-reduce job
- addInputPath: takes a JobContext and a Path as input and adds the path to the list of inputs for the map-reduce job

DEPENDENCIES:
- This class depends on the Configuration class from the Hadoop framework.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY: Represents a section of an input file to be processed by an InputFormat.
CORE LOGIC: None
INPUTS/OUTPUTS: None
DEPENDENCIES: None

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The main purpose of this chunk of code is to define the FixedLengthInputFormat class, which is used to read input files containing fixed length records.

CORE LOGIC:

The code defines the FixedLengthInputFormat class, which extends the FileInputFormat class. It overrides the createRecordReader() method to return a FixedLengthRecordReader object, which is used to read the fixed length records from the input file.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The FixedLengthInputFormat class takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as input parameters in the createRecordReader() method. It returns a RecordReader object, which is used to read the fixed length records from the input file.

DEPENDENCIES:

The FixedLengthInputFormat class depends on the following classes:

FileInputFormat: This class provides the basic framework for reading input files.

CompressionCodecFactory: This class is used to determine the compression codec of a file.

FixedLengthRecordReader: This class is used to read the fixed length records from the input file.

The FixedLengthInputFormat class also depends on the following configuration properties:

FIXED_RECORD_LENGTH: This property is used to set the length of each record. It is set using the setRecordLength() method.

The FixedLengthInputFormat class also depends on the following methods:

isSplitable(): This method is used to determine whether a file is splitable or not. It returns true if the file is not compressed, and false otherwise.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- FixedLengthRecordReader is a RecordReader that reads fixed-length records from an input split.
- It returns the record offset as the key and the record as bytes in the value.

CORE LOGIC:
- The initialize method initializes the reader by setting the start and end positions of the split, and opening the input file.
- The nextKeyValue method reads the next record from the input stream and returns it as a key-value pair.
- The getProgress method calculates the progress of the reader by comparing the current position to the start and end positions of the split.
- The close method closes the input stream and returns the decompressor to the pool.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The initialize method takes a Configuration object, a split start position, a split length, and a Path object as input.
- The nextKeyValue method returns a LongWritable key and a BytesWritable value.
- The getProgress method returns a float value between 0 and 1 representing the progress of the reader.
- The close method does not take any input and does not return any output.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The FixedLengthRecordReader class depends on the Configuration, Path, FSDataInputStream, Seekable, CompressionCodec, CompressionInputStream, and Decompressor classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk of code is to handle and report a list of problems with the input.

CORE LOGIC:

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a list of problems as input and provides a method to get the complete list of problems reported.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce library and the Java IOException class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This class converts the input keys and values to their String forms by calling toString() method.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this class is to read key/value pairs from a SequenceFile and convert the keys and values to their String forms using the toString() method.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code implements a filter for sequence files. It allows a map/reduce job to work on a sample of sequence files, decided by the filter class set by the job.

CORE LOGIC:
The core logic of this code is the Filter class, which is an interface that defines a filter function. The filter function decides whether a record should be filtered or not based on the record key. The code also includes three implementations of the Filter interface: RegexFilter, PercentFilter, and MD5Filter.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes an InputSplit and a TaskAttemptContext as inputs and returns a RecordReader as an output. The RecordReader reads records from the input split and applies the filter to each record.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.lib.input package and the org.apache.hadoop.io package. It also uses the Configuration class from the org.apache.hadoop.conf package and the ReflectionUtils class from the org.apache.hadoop.util package.
